programmes.show_description: Hundreds of women in the UK become victims of revenge porn and deepfake imagery every year, but the chance of conviction for these crimes is very low. This edition of the Exposure strand is about three women forced to take matters into their own hands after finding themselves portrayed as offering sexual services in faked profiles, followingtheir five-year hunt for answers and for justice, as the women unravel something far bigger than they could have imagined and the one man at its heart
News, sport and weather from across Scotland

Jeremy is joined by good friend and food critic Grace Dent as they kick off a culinary adventure with a cocktail that is infused with beef dripping before they head to one of Hong Kong's finest restaurants. Jeremy then arranges a journey to Kuk Po, one of Hong Kong's last traditional villages, where he and Grace feast on true local cuisine

Stephen Mulhern hosts another charity edition of the game show, with Les Dennis, Rebecca Sarker and Jordan Stephens trying to say what they see as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen good causes

The fast-paced game of words, hosted by Adil Ray, where three pairs of contestants face Lingo grids, fiendish puzzlewords, and a tense head-to-head round as they battle to reach the final and a chance to win a 15,000-pound jackpot. Taking part alongside the reigning champions are nan and granddaughter Diane and Becky, and mother and son Liz and Adam

Ranvir Singh hosts the quiz where contestants take on Riddlemaster Henry Lewis in an attempt to solve a series of riddles - packed full of confusing conundrums and quick-fire general knowledge questions, only one team can make it through to the high-stakes final of Henry's Riddle Run and earn the chance to win an exciting cash prize as they take on a series of rebus riddles against the clock. How will Joel and Sara, Jazz and Jem, and Jordan-Leigh and Sam cope under pressure?

Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

Bradley Walsh hosts the show where thousands of pounds are at stake. All that stands between Heather from St Ives, Andy from Canterbury, Felicity from Dunfermline, Ben from Wirral and that money is the small matter of one of the country's finest quiz brains

programmes.show_name: Spring Clean: Keep, Chuck or Cash In? Tonight
programmes.show_description: Nearly half of the UK is living with stress-inducing clutter, but are the unwanted items lying around homes trash or treasure? The team investigates why many find it difficult to have a clear out, why having too much clutter can be bad for the health, and what viewers could upcycle or exchange for cash

programmes.show_name: Gary Barlow's Wine Tour: South Africa
programmes.show_description: The Take That veteran heads into Johannesburg to meet soul sensation and friend Mica Paris. The pair spend time at Sanctuary Mandela, taking in the history of the great man, hearing live music, and sampling fantastic food, before heading to Soweto for a community braai, an event featuring great wine and entertainment

programmes.show_name: Supermarket Prices: Does Loyalty Pay? - Tonight
programmes.show_description: Food prices have surged in recent years and are now almost 40 per cent higher than at the end of 2020. So what can we do to cut the cost of our weekly shop, and do loyalty schemes and in-store discounts really deliver? Kate Quilton reports
